title: erysimum × cheiri
intro: Erysimum × cheiri, also known as wallflower, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae and is a cross between Erysimum cheiri and Erysimum crepidifolium. Traditionally, wallflower has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of wallflower include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve skin conditions. Wallflower can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its usage and preparation may vary depending on the region and intended application.
